steps.title: Subtract matching-base exponents
steps.content:
When powers with the same base are divided, subtract the exponents. Apply that rule separately to $h$ and $q$.
$$\frac{h^{16}q^8}{h^4q^{24}}=h^{16-4}q^{8-24}$$
$$=h^{12}q^{-16}$$

steps.title: Move the negative exponent
steps.content:
A negative exponent means the factor belongs in the denominator.
$$q^{-16}=\frac{1}{q^{16}}$$
So the expression becomes
$$\frac{h^{12}}{q^{16}}$$

steps.title: Match the expression
steps.content: Choice C leaves $q^{16}$ in the numerator, and choices B and D use the wrong exponents. The simplified expression matches choice A. The answer is A.
